mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month increases in industrial production reflect robust manufacturing output and aggregate supply, which supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If INDPRO increases but SPX 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of instances over a 3-year rolling window, or if the correlation between INDPRO changes and SPX returns is not significantly positive.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the federal funds rate by at least 25 basis points in a single month signals an easing cycle, reducing discount rates and boosting equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS declines by at least 0.25% in a single month
failure_condition: If easing events are followed by negative 20-day returns in more than 40% of cases over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint positive month-over-month growth in payroll employment and retail sales over two consecutive months signals strong consumer demand and income, driving corporate profits and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: both PAYEMS and RSAFS show positive month-over-month growth for two consecutive months
failure_condition: If joint positive growth does not lead to significantly positive forward 60-day returns (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year CPI growth exceeds 2%, a sequential increase in the federal funds rate signals monetary tightening that depresses equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If tightening signals (FEDFUNDS increase when CPI>2% YoY) are followed by positive 5-day returns in more than 30% of instances, or the average negative return is not statistically significant.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate below 5% signals a late-cycle economy with overheating risks, leading to anticipation of monetary tightening and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If UNRATE below 5% is followed by positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint positive month-over-month growth in housing starts and industrial production over two consecutive months indicates robust construction and manufacturing activity, which boosts aggregate demand and leads to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: both HOUST and INDPRO show positive month-over-month growth for two consecutive months
failure_condition: If joint positive growth does not predict positive 20-day returns with rank IC >= 0.05 over a 2-year rolling window, or the average return is not significantly positive.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate is already below 5%, a further sequential decline signals tightening labor market conditions and wage pressure that erode corporate margins, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If declines in UNRATE when below 5% are followed by positive 20-day returns in more than 40% of instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A large month-over-month increase in the CPI (exceeding 0.4%) signals rising inflation pressure, prompting bond market sell-offs and a rotation away from equities, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If high CPI months (MoM>0.4%) are followed by positive 5-day returns in more than 50% of cases, or if the average negative return is not statistically significant over a 5-year window.
